subject: Adding guard PIP
commit_message:
Adding guard PIP

Adding the PIP support into the translator to setup the count
attribute as an attribute generated by PIP and not sent by
calling application.

Added JUnit for OperationsHistoryPipEngine.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1471
Change-Id: I71773ee8ea0e6c02873506d973a604899383d4b5
Signed-off-by: Pamela Dragosh <pdragosh@research.att.com>

Patch Set 5:

The JUnit is failing, will continue to look into it. Josh I added in your JUnit, please look at what I did.
1) JPA is weird on where it looks for perisistence.xml. It doesn't know its being run by a JUnit - that's why we need to configure it via properties. Properties allow us to change scenario based on circumstances. eg JUnit for the class, JUnit for the guard application, JUnit for production/runtime.
2) I created the OnapOperationsHistoryDbao for a purpose - so we don't have "select ..." strings all over the place.

Patch Set 5:

My switch to use TIMESTAMPDIFF from date_sub is not working. H2 doesn't support date_sub which is why I switched. But timestampdiff returns integer values, not a date. Oops. I am in the process of fixing that now.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Patch Set 6:

TIMESTAMPADD should be compatible between Mysql and H2. But I'm still getting only 0 values back. Will work on this more later today. Unless Josh you have time to debug this?

Patch Set 6:

I'm PTO today, but will try to take a quick look. I have already noticed one issue with the H2 version TIMESTAMPADD (it doesn't appear to support "seconds" as a time interval type).

Patch Set 6:

If you modify the core query in doDatabaseQuery (changes inline below) to a more standard >= comparison, it should fix things (in local tests, doDatabaseQuery is reporting 1 when called the second time and testGetCountFromDb is passing. The way you've written the query looks like it ought to work, perhaps the reason why it is failing is that H2 uses a different dialect. If you prefer not using the >= operator in your query or are curious to determine the root problem, the first thing I'd suggest is adding 

<property name="hibernate.dialect" value="org.hibernate.dialect.H2Dialect"/>
            <property name="hibernate.hbm2ddl.auto" value="validate"/>

to the test persistence.xml (see https://memorynotfound.com/unit-test-jpa-junit-in-memory-h2-database/ for details).

I probably won't be online after this until Monday, I'll check back in then.

diff --git a/applications/common/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/pdp/xacml/application/common/OnapOperationsHistoryPipEngine.java b/appli
cations/common/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/pdp/xacml/application/common/OnapOperationsHistoryPipEngine.java
index 4d3667d..7536e88 100644
--- a/applications/common/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/pdp/xacml/application/common/OnapOperationsHistoryPipEngine.java
+++ b/applications/common/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/pdp/xacml/application/common/OnapOperationsHistoryPipEngine.java
@@ -305,10 +305,9 @@ public class OnapOperationsHistoryPipEngine extends StdConfigurableEngine {
                     + " and actor=?"
                     + " and operation=?"
                     + " and target=?"
-                    + " and endtime between TIMESTAMPADD("
+                    + " and endtime >= TIMESTAMPADD("
                     + timeWindowScale.toUpperCase()
-                    + ", ?, CURRENT_DATE)"
-                    + " and CURRENT_DATE";
+                    + ", ?, CURRENT_DATE)";
             //
             // We are expecting a single result
             //
Uploaded patch set 7.

Patch Set 8:

min/max is failing - probably because of my changes for test5.

Patch Set 8:

(3 comments)

min/max is failing b/c tests are currently independent. specifically, in test3 a frequency guard is loaded. When test4 runs it adds a second min/max guard, but the frequency guard evaluates to deny and the root combining algorithm used is permit-unless-deny, so deny results and test4 fails.

Patch Set 8:

typo in previous reply "are currently independent" => "aren't currently independent"

Patch Set 8:

Josh thanks - I actually want each jUnit to build upon each other. As each use case adds guard policy, it shouldn't break the others. The scale out use cases uses BOTH the frequency and the min/max. So they should work.

Patch Set 8:

Ah, very welcome, got it. In that case, the important point is that test4 is currently failing on the frequency guard, not the minmax guard (I modified the test locally and confirmed that test4 passed when only the minmax guard is loaded). That info should help you restructure, though it strikes me as a bit tricky since the frequency guard is going to deny anytime you've got 1 or more successful actions and the minmax is going to deny anytime you've got less than 1 successful action. Thinking out loud, I guess one option is changing the frequency guard to have a limit of 2 instead of 1, calling insertOperationEvent twice, running test3, clearing the db (but not the PDP) after test 3, then and calling insertOperationEvent once in test4. Anyway, I'll stay posted for additional code submssions, etc.

Patch Set 8:

Ok thanks for helping to debug this - I won't get to this until later today. If you think you can help me out in the next few hours let me know and I'll hold off until then. I would like to get this code merged by COB today so we can connect the E2E from the REST through.

After that, I won't be touching this code again unless requested.
